paragraphs.text: Dear Sir/Madam, I would like to express my support for the cause of preserving video games and putting an end to the practice of “killing” games, making it impossible for players who bought them to access them. Personally, I discovered the Anthem game several years ago, first on PCs. Faced with the many criticisms, I didn’t play immediately, because the studio had promised a major overhaul, as was already the case on other titles, but unfortunately never arrived. More recently, jai bought Anthem from Xbox Series X, during my transition from PC gaming to this console. However, between my disease and seasonal candles, it is difficult to spend time on video games. This summer, I’ve learned that I should end the game in less than six months, as from mid-January, solo mode will no longer be available. This is particularly frustrating given that a simple patch could deactivate the connection to the servers while allowing the game to function. This solution has been applied to other games, showing that there is an alternative that is respectful of the player. I believe it is important that studios become aware of the real commitment of players to their games and find lasting ways to maintain access even after the official end of the medium. Thank you for taking this testimony into account, which reflects the concerns of many passionate players who are concerned about the sustainability of their leisure activities. Warmly,
